planningNotes: Plan: cycle 116 closes the REVIEW.md save/resume priority with an explicit saved-run resume UX, route-bound checkpoint restore, and persisted checkpoint-performance evidence.

Breakdown: preserve the cycle 85 through 115 route, map, material-breakup, collision-authoring, restore-execution, session-audio, scope-calibration, concealment, CSM-profile, reflection-probe, packaging-validation, tester-delivery, lighting-architecture, automated-capture, LOD-switching, time-of-day, Forward+ start, scoped-safe AA, physical-atmosphere, shadow indirect rendering, SDF UI, SSR/IBL, render graph, authored audio mix, firing feedback, group-NPC patrol state, district texture closeout, multi-route binding, scope optics, and collision workflow while promoting the review card into a resume-safe saved session.

Reference: carry the cycle 28 group-observer baseline, cycle 29 observer-audio feedback, cycle 30 mission hooks, cycles 31 through 50 alternate-route staging, cycle 51 collision-authoring readiness, cycles 52 through 57 environmental fidelity work, cycles 58 through 78 restore safety work, cycles 79 through 115 weapon, route, material, profiling, packaging, tester, lighting, capture, LOD, time-of-day, Forward+, AA, atmosphere, indirect-rendering, SDF UI, SSR, render-graph, audio-mix, firing-feedback, group-NPC, texture, multi-route, scope-optics, and collision-authoring decisions, then add cycle 116 save/resume as the eighth next-priority recovery cycle.

timeOfDay.rule: derive renderer sun direction, sky colors, fog tint, haze, ambient/diffuse intensity, and shadow coverage from authored scenario time
timeOfDay.label: 16:45 late-summer low sun

shadow.profileStatus: CSM readiness profile active; single shadow map remains the shipping path
shadow.profileRule: profile scoped coverage, split targets, bias, sector count, blocker count, and frame timing before allocating cascade textures

antiAliasing.rule: smooth high-contrast scene edges in postprocess while rejecting blends across depth discontinuities
antiAliasing.scopeStabilityRule: avoid temporal history and reject cross-depth blends so scoped skyline silhouettes do not ghost

physicalAtmosphere.rule: derive sky color, horizon lift, and distance haze from time-of-day sun elevation plus scene-authored Rayleigh/Mie controls
physicalAtmosphere.scopeStabilityRule: use deterministic camera and sun vectors so scoped sky silhouettes remain stable between frames

indirectRendering.rule: encode shadow-caster object draw commands through a per-frame MTLIndirectCommandBuffer before expanding to material draws
indirectRendering.coverageNote: covers shadow-casting scene drawables; terrain and material draws remain direct
indirectRendering.fallbackRule: fall back to direct draw loop if ICB allocation or capacity validation fails
indirectRendering.measurementRule: use frame timing, drawable count, and capture-review screenshots to compare scoped shadow stability before broader adoption

sdfUI.status: SDF UI rendering active for HUD, scope, and map labels
sdfUI.rule: render critical text through a scalable signed-distance-style outline and shadow pass before a generated atlas is required
sdfUI.mode: SwiftUI vector text with SDF-style outline pass
sdfUI.mapLabelRule: sector, road, and north labels keep outline contrast across canvas scale and route overlays
sdfUI.scopeRule: scope status, instruction, presentation, and shot cadence labels keep outlined readability over aperture and reticle
sdfUI.fallbackRule: system vector text remains the fallback until a generated MSDF atlas is introduced
sdfUI.measurementRule: capture HUD, scope, and map text at desktop review size and verify no label clipping or blur regressions

renderGraph.rule: represent the current shadow, scene, and presentation passes as graph nodes with explicit resource reads and writes
renderGraph.aliasingRule: keep transient resources unaliased until graph lifetime validation is expanded
renderGraph.validationRule: assert every pass reads only imported resources or resources written by earlier graph passes
renderGraph.expansionRule: promote CSM, SSR material masks, SSAO, capture, and future volumetric passes into graph nodes before adding new manual sequencing

audioMix.rule: route audio uses explicit category gains for ambience, footsteps, scope toggles, weapon feedback, and observer alerts
audioMix.mixRule: ambient bed stays below movement and observer cues; scope and weapon cues remain clear during review captures
audioMix.smokeRule: settings expose master audio gain, Session Audio reports cue state, and Audio Mix reports scene/user category levels

environmentalMotion.status: Scope and concealment polish active; West Basin water and vegetation closeout retained
environmentalMotion.rule: drive vegetation screening, traversal rustle cues, shoreline, and lake-surface motion from scene-authored wind controls

materialBreakup.status: West Basin shoreline material closeout active; route binding unchanged
materialBreakup.rule: use source-backed terrain, promenade, facade, road, and water material assignments on West Basin and Yarralumla without changing collision or route markers

surfaceFidelity.status: Scope calibration and vegetation concealment closeout active
surfaceFidelity.rule: review scope range/drop/hold/parallax cues together with West Basin vegetation response, shoreline ripple, lake water material response, promenade hardscape, road decals, and district facade breakup

distantLOD.rule: switch configured landmark graybox drawables from full geometry to 6-vertex impostor cards past the authored threshold
distantLOD.scopeStabilityRule: scope mode uses the same measured impostor threshold so skyline pop can be captured and reviewed

waterReflection.status: SSR with IBL probe fallback active
waterReflection.rule: blend bounded screen-space reflection samples with a sky-probe fallback for West Basin water and glass readability
waterReflection.approach: bounded postprocess SSR with static local sky-probe fallback
waterReflection.deferredReason: full material-masked SSR waits for render graph and material ID buffers

packagingAutomation.rule: release packages must pass manifest validation, version policy, archive naming, and package-content smoke checks before tester sharing
manifestChecks: all sector files resolve and lint

testerDistribution.status: tester distribution pipeline scripted; notarization remains credential-gated
testerDistribution.rule: tester builds require validation, current handoff docs, notarization readiness notes, and SDF UI readability checks before sharing
testerDistribution.channel: local-review zip with documented notarization handoff
testerDistribution.notarizationStatus: notarytool check scripted; submission waits for Apple credentials and Metal release toolchain
testerDistribution.ciPlan: CI gate runs validate-only and distribution check before package upload
deliveryChecklist: validate world manifest and release docs
build/package signed release when Metal toolchain is installed
share zip plus tester distribution guide
collect startup, route, scope, map, and packaging feedback
testerDistribution.sdfUIPlan: Cycle 106 SDF-style UI path active for HUD, scope, and map label crispness

lightingArchitecture.status: Save/resume closeout active; collision authoring, scope optics, multi-route playability, district texture closeout, group NPC behavior, firing feedback, authored audio mix, render graph, SSR/IBL, SDF UI, shadow indirect rendering, physical atmosphere, and Forward+ retained
lightingArchitecture.rule: use authored scenario time for the renderer sun, apply physical atmosphere controls to sky/fog, keep bounded local diagnostic lights, execute shadow-caster object draws through the indirect command path, render critical UI text through the scalable SDF-style path, blend lake/glass reflections through SSR with probe fallback, validate renderer passes through a frame graph descriptor, mix route audio through scene-authored category gains, present scoped rifle shots with visible flash/recoil/result feedback, drive authored patrol pairs with runtime formation movement plus alert halt/resume behavior, keep Black Mountain/West Basin material reads source-backed through the shared Canberra texture library, bind any authored rehearsal route selected at briefing before live movement begins, render the 4x optic with lens dirt, edge aberration, calibrated mil spacing, parallax compensation, expose selectable collision blocker review with validation/export guidance, and resume saved checkpoint progress only through explicit guarded user action
lightingArchitecture.sunPolicy: scenario time controls the single renderer sun while dynamic point lights layer local light contribution from cycle 102
lightingArchitecture.atmospherePolicy: time-of-day config feeds physical atmosphere sky color, horizon lift, fog tint, and distance haze from cycle 104
lightingArchitecture.indirectRenderingPolicy: cycle 105 uses a per-frame MTLIndirectCommandBuffer for shadow-casting object commands with direct-draw fallback
lightingArchitecture.clusteredLightingDecision: Forward+ start active: CPU-side drawable light selection feeds up to four local lights to the object shader
lightingArchitecture.renderGraphDecision: frame graph scaffold active for the current shadow, scene geometry, and presentation postprocess passes; resource aliasing and fuller scheduling remain expansion work
measuredPrerequisites: frame timing line remains visible
CSM profile reports cascade prerequisites
LOD/reflection line reports shimmer blockers
tester packaging gates remain scripted
scope skyline readability remains stable
time-of-day HUD line reports scenario hour, sun angle, ambient/diffuse light, and shadow multiplier
Forward+ lighting summary reports diagnostic light count and CPU cluster tags
anti-aliasing line reports edge threshold, blend strength, depth rejection, and scoped stability rule
physical atmosphere line reports Rayleigh, Mie, ozone, turbidity, and density controls
indirect rendering line reports ICB mode, draw class, capacity, coverage, and fallback scope
SDF UI line reports text mode, HUD/scope/map coverage, outline, shadow, and minimum scale
Water Reflection line reports active SSR strength and probe fallback strength
Render Graph line reports pass count, transient/imported resources, and graph pass order
Audio Mix line reports scene-authored category gains and persisted user master gain
Shot Feedback line reports visible recoil and hit, blocker, ground, or clear-miss result
Patrol Pairs line reports active formation members, moving count, route, roles, spacing, and live sweep offset
Black Mountain Materials and West Basin Materials lines report Cycle 112 source-backed acceptance framing
Route Selection line reports all three playable rehearsal routes can bind from briefing
Scope Calibration line reports calibrated mil spacing and compensated parallax
Collision Selection, Collision Validation, and Collision Export lines report selected blocker authoring workflow state
Last Session, Review Resume, and Checkpoint Performance lines report persisted route-bound resume state

sessionPersistence.rule: persist route identity, checkpoint progress, checkpoint performance, map/scope review state, and resume only after identity, freshness, target, and explicit user intent checks pass

route.name: Canberra Combat-Lane Rehearsal
route.summary: Follow one authored line from Woden to Belconnen while scoped rifle drift, grouped observer relays, alert memory, observer audio, mission-script hooks, active-route loader staging, alternate-route metrics, binding-gate validation, selection locking, activation guarding, rollback guarding, commit gating, dry-run comparison, handoff planning, checkpoint ownership, and contact-lane cues validate that the atlas can carry route expansion without losing Canberra readability.

reviewPack.title: Canberra Review Pack Baseline
reviewPack.summary: Reference-backed Canberra atlas baseline with Cycle 116 save/resume closeout, Cycle 115 collision authoring workflow, Cycle 114 scope optics closeout, Cycle 113 primary and alternate rehearsal routes selectable from briefing, and Cycle 112 Black Mountain, Bruce, Belconnen, West Basin, Yarralumla, and shoreline material closeout coverage.
reviewPack.captureFormat: Use Tools/capture_review.sh for scripted title, live-route, overhead-map, scoped-view, and pause-shell captures; use --baseline to produce review diffs.
openRisks: All three rehearsal routes are now selectable and live-bindable at fresh-run boundaries; Cycle 123 still needs formal minimap accuracy verification across every route.
West Basin reflections remain material-and-motion based until the later reflection probe cycle.

Comparison capture flow is scripted for the canonical review shell states; Cycle 112 adds district acceptance framing notes for Black Mountain and West Basin but does not automate camera steering to those exact viewpoints.

comparisonStops.captureNote: Raise the scope and frame the Woden towers above Hindmarsh Drive.
Verify State Circle, Adelaide Avenue, and Sydney Avenue as one handoff.
Hold the lake edge, Kings Avenue approach, and eastern ridge in one frame.
Keep Constitution Avenue and Kings Avenue readable together.
Open the map and confirm Northbourne, Alinga, and Civic massing without narration.

comparisonStops.sourceFocus: Cycle 112 source-backed West Basin shoreline, water, vegetation, road, and facade material closeout
comparisonStops.captureNote: Capture Commonwealth Avenue west, West Basin water motion, promenade hardscape, Yarralumla vegetation response, asphalt texture, and facade breakup in one frame.

comparisonStops.sourceFocus: Cycle 112 source-backed Black Mountain/Telstra/Bruce/Belconnen material closeout
comparisonStops.captureNote: Raise the scope and frame Telstra concrete, Black Mountain dry-grass slopes, Bruce/AIS facade breakup, and Belconnen/Ginninderra support masses together.
Keep Belconnen Way, the interchange, and the town centre towers legible in one pass.
Finish on the northern lake edge and Ginninderra Drive exit read.

combatRehearsal.title: Canberra Cycle 89 Third Rehearsal Route Authoring
combatRehearsal.summary: Live Canberra contact pass with a third authored rehearsal route, multi-route map previews, new north-west threat geometry, route-readiness metadata, West Basin shoreline material assignments, water material response, vegetation wind response, West Basin capture notes, Black Mountain/Telstra/Bruce source-backed material assignments, Black Mountain material capture IDs, lead/wing roles, formation spacing validation, paired observer relays, expanded alert audio, scoped mil-dot reticle ticks, holdover and parallax optic text, recoil recovery reporting, crack-thump combatRehearsal.exposureGuide: Open ground is intentional; move between named cover screens before raising the optic on skyline-heavy checkpoints.
combatRehearsal.recoveryRule: If suspicion spikes, break line of sight, re-anchor on the nearest cover screen, and retry the current lane from the latest checkpoint rather than freelancing a shortcut.

contactStops.coverHint: Use Woden Interchange Screen, then settle behind Woden Scope Wall before raising the optic.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Reset behind Woden Scope Wall if the towers stay exposed.

contactStops.coverHint: Treat State Circle Median as the break point before crossing the approach.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Drop back to State Circle Median before re-entering the avenue handoff.

contactStops.coverHint: Use Kings Avenue Screen before stepping into the east-basin transfer.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Re-enter from the Kings Avenue Screen instead of pushing the open shoulder.

contactStops.coverHint: Use East Basin Survey Wall before holding the shoreline and ridge in scope.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Reset to East Basin Survey Wall if the lookout stays hot.

contactStops.coverHint: Constitution Axis Screen is the last safe stop before the basin-core crossing.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Recover behind Constitution Axis Screen and restart the crossing from there.

contactStops.coverHint: Use Civic Interchange Screen before checking the Northbourne and Alinga read.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Let the grid cool from Civic Interchange Screen before another peek.

contactStops.coverHint: West Basin Screen protects the embankment handoff before the shoreline opens.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Recover to West Basin Screen before pushing the promenade again.

contactStops.coverHint: Use Tower Footing Screen before exposing the Telstra Tower perch.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Drop to Tower Footing Screen if the Bruce saddle stays watched.

contactStops.coverHint: Belconnen Median Screen is the safe pause before the interchange towers.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Reset to Belconnen Median Screen before re-entering the town-centre lane.

contactStops.coverHint: Use Ginninderra Access Screen before finishing on the northern exit read.
contactStops.recoveryNote: Break contact at Ginninderra Access Screen, then finish the lane on the retry.

missionScript.title: Cycle 30 Checkpoint Mission Hooks
missionScript.summary: Data-driven phase hooks annotate key Woden-to-Belconnen checkpoints with objective, trigger, success, and failure cues ahead of later mission scripting.

phases.objective: Confirm the rehearsal start and orient on the southern basin lane.
phases.trigger: Start shell confirm or first checkpoint touch.
phases.successCue: Route handoff is live and the next contact lane is Woden Scope Perch.
phases.failureCue: If the route state is unclear, return to briefing and restart from the authored start.

phases.objective: Raise the scope behind cover and identify the paired Woden observers.
phases.trigger: Enter the perch radius with observer pressure active.
phases.successCue: Woden contact read is established; proceed toward State Circle after cover recovery.
phases.failureCue: If shared alert pressure rises, break sight behind Woden Scope Wall before retrying.

phases.objective: Cross the Parliament south approach without losing the atlas line.
phases.trigger: Reach State Circle while suspicion is below the failure threshold.
phases.successCue: Parliament approach is clear enough to hand off toward Kings Avenue.
phases.failureCue: Recover to State Circle Median and re-enter the transfer after line of sight drops.

phases.objective: Validate high-exposure shoreline observer audio and scoped skyline framing.
phases.trigger: Enter the East Basin lookout radius and hold a scope-ready stance.
phases.successCue: East Basin exposure and audio feedback are readable without collapsing route flow.
phases.failureCue: Reset to East Basin Survey Wall and wait for observer memory to cool.

phases.objective: Confirm Civic observer-state feedback while preserving the named-road atlas read.
phases.trigger: Reach Civic after completing the Constitution Axis handoff.
phases.successCue: Civic relay state is visible in HUD and map; continue to West Basin.
phases.failureCue: Use the Civic Arcade Screen and retry once suspicion is cooling.

phases.objective: Use the 4x optic to frame Black Mountain while checking mission and threat overlays.
phases.trigger: Reach the scope perch and raise the optic on the north-west lane.
phases.successCue: Black Mountain overwatch remains readable with mission and observer feedback active.
phases.failureCue: Drop to Tower Footing Screen and reacquire the route from cover.

phases.objective: Validate paired Belconnen observer pressure before the final exit lane.
phases.trigger: Enter Belconnen Town Centre Review after the Belconnen Way overlook.
phases.successCue: Belconnen town-centre pressure is staged and the final Ginninderra hook is armed.
phases.failureCue: Recover to Belconnen Median Screen before re-entering the town-centre lane.

phases.objective: Complete the route with mission, contact, and atlas summaries still aligned.
phases.trigger: Touch the final Ginninderra Drive Review marker.
phases.successCue: Mission hook route complete; ready for alternate-route selection.
phases.failureCue: Break contact at Ginninderra Access Screen and retry the final lane.

routeSelection.loaderStatus: briefing route selector can bind primary plus two alternate routes
routeSelection.validationStatus: three rehearsal routes eligible for live map accuracy review
routeSelection.validationRule: requires 3+ markers, 100m+ planned distance, and 2+ sectors
routeSelection.selectionStatus: briefing selector cycles primary and both alternate routes
routeSelection.selectionRule: route choice may change only from briefing before a run starts
routeSelection.activationStatus: fresh-run activation available for all authored routes
routeSelection.activationRule: selected route activates only from the title briefing before live movement
routeSelection.rollbackStatus: rollback guard armed; primary route remains recoverable
routeSelection.rollbackRule: failed alternate binding returns to the primary route before checkpoint order changes
routeSelection.commitStatus: commit gate live with route footer verification
routeSelection.commitRule: commit selected route only before live movement input begins, then verify map markers and route footer agree
routeSelection.dryRunStatus: dry run promoted into live selector
routeSelection.dryRunRule: compare all three checkpoint orders through briefing selection without mutating mid-run
routeSelection.promotionStatus: promotion complete for both authored alternates
routeSelection.promotionRule: promoted alternates remain bounded to fresh-run route binding
routeSelection.auditStatus: promotion audit passed for primary plus two alternate routes
routeSelection.auditRule: audit must confirm active route id, checkpoint count, and restart boundary after each selection
routeSelection.boundaryRule: route changes remain locked to briefing or fresh-run restart boundaries
routeSelection.armingRule: selector records the next route without rebinding current live checkpoints
routeSelection.confirmationRule: Start Demo confirms the selected route and binds its checkpoints before movement
routeSelection.releaseStatus: multi-route playability released for local review
routeSelection.releaseRule: release keeps primary rollback by returning to briefing and selecting the primary route
routeSelection.preflightStatus: live-switch preflight passed for all authored routes
routeSelection.preflightRule: preflight validates each route before checkpoint order is rebound
routeSelection.handoffStatus: restart-safe handoff implemented for all authored routes
routeSelection.handoffRule: bind selected route only from briefing or restart boundary

collisionAuthoring.rule: select blocker footprints on the overhead map, review validation, and copy source IDs before JSON edit/export
collisionAuthoring.audit: selected blocker, sector ownership, dimensions, clearance, and export guidance are surfaced beside route preflight metadata
collisionAuthoring.blockerScope: graybox blocker volumes and authored sector collision volumes
collisionAuthoring.validationStatus: selected blocker reports dimensions, sector ownership, source tier, and minimum route clearance
collisionAuthoring.exportStatus: copy selected blocker source id before editing sector collision JSON
collisionAuthoring.reviewGuidance: cycle every blocker footprint, check selected highlight and dimensions, then update the owning sector JSON

alternateRoutes.name: East Basin To Belconnen Probe
alternateRoutes.summary: Second playable rehearsal route that starts from the east-basin lookout, cuts through Civic and Black Mountain, then rejoins the Belconnen endgame.
alternateRoutes.activationRule: select from briefing, then Start Demo binds this checkpoint order

alternateRoutes.name: West Basin To Ginninderra Shore Thread
alternateRoutes.summary: Third playable rehearsal route that starts at the West Basin promenade, threads the Black Mountain western shoulder, pressures the Belconnen Way handoff, and exits along Ginninderra with distinct north-west watcher coverage.
alternateRoutes.activationRule: select from briefing, then Start Demo binds this checkpoint order
